# prompts.md — AI Coding Prompts
# Copy-paste these into your AI tools to build BeatDrop
# Generated by DocForge • 2026-06-07

---

## How to Use This File

- Prompts are ordered by build sequence — go top to bottom.
- Each prompt is tagged with the recommended AI tool:
  🟣 CLAUDE CODE — for complex multi-file generation, refactoring, architecture
  🔵 CURSOR — for inline edits, file modifications, quick changes
  🟢 GEMINI — for brainstorming alternatives, research, quick questions

---

## PHASE 1: Desktop App Scaffolding & Setup

### Prompt 1.1 — Project Scaffolding 🟣 CLAUDE CODE
```text
Set up a Tauri v2 desktop application workspace.
Use Vite, React, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and Lucide React.
Create custom window controls (Minimize, Maximize, Close) and hide the default native OS window frame.
Ensure the base window background is set to `#09090B` (zinc-950/midnight).

Context:
- System: Windows OS
- Package Manager: npm
```

### Prompt 1.2 — Rust Downloader Engine 🟣 CLAUDE CODE
```text
Write a Tauri command in Rust `download_track(url: String)` that triggers a yt-dlp sidecar binary to download audio as an MP3/M4A file.
1. Create validation using regex to ensure the URL is a valid YouTube video.
2. Spawn the packaged `yt-dlp` sidecar located in `src-tauri/bin/` with arguments: `-f bestaudio -x --audio-format mp3 --embed-thumbnail -o "%(id)s.%(ext)s"`.
3. Capture the sidecar's stdout stream to parse download progress percentage (e.g. "[download] 42% of...") and emit custom progress events `download-progress` back to the frontend.
4. On success, parse the metadata of the output file (title, duration) and write the track entry into our local SQLite database.

Files to reference:
- src-tauri/src/commands/downloader.rs
- src-tauri/src/main.rs
```

### Prompt 1.3 — Database Integrator 🟣 CLAUDE CODE
```text
Configure a local SQLite database in the Tauri Rust backend.
1. Automatically run migrations on app start to create tables: `tracks`, `playlists`, and `playlist_tracks` (include indexes on date_added).
2. Create Rust handlers to fetch all tracks, save a new track, and manage playlist associations.
3. Expose Tauri commands: `get_all_tracks()`, `create_playlist(name: string)`, and `add_track_to_playlist(playlist_id, track_id)`.
```

### Prompt 1.4 — Cyber Audio Interface 🟣 CLAUDE CODE
```text
Build a premium, single-page React frontend using Tailwind CSS for BeatDrop.
Apply the 'Cyber Neon Violet' theme:
- Colors: Midnight Zn-950 (`#09090B`), Deep Violet card backgrounds (`rgba(30, 27, 75, 0.4)` with `backdrop-blur`), vibrant violet accents (`#8B5CF6`), and glowing cyan borders (`#06B6D4`).
- Components:
  1. Sidebar containing App title, Navigation (Library, Downloader), and Playlists section.
  2. Main Content panel displaying the Library grid (song cards with hover states) or the Downloader input interface.
  3. Bottom Player Bar with persistent audio controls, progress bar slider, volume controller, and current song cover art.
- Bind the player commands (play, pause, seek, volume change) to a hidden HTML `<audio>` tag that loads local tracks using Tauri's custom file protocol handler.
```

---

## PHASE 2: Mobile Port & WiFi Sync (Flutter)

### Prompt 2.1 — Flutter Offline Player Setup 🟣 CLAUDE CODE
```text
Initialize a new Flutter application built for offline audio playback.
1. Use `sqflite` to create the SQLite client holding tables matching our schema: `tracks`, `playlists`, `playlist_tracks`.
2. Integrate the `just_audio` and `audio_service` plugins to handle playback, controls, and background playback services with system notifications.
3. Build the UI following the Cyber Neon dark-mode theme, featuring a Library tab, a Mini Player bar, and a Full Screen Player sliding card.
```

### Prompt 2.2 — Rust Sync Server (Tauri Side) 🟣 CLAUDE CODE
```text
Write a P2P local network sync module in the Rust backend of the Tauri application.
1. When sync mode is turned on, run a lightweight Axum HTTP server on a random port.
2. Expose endpoint `GET /sync/manifest` returning list of stored tracks (IDs, file hashes).
3. Expose endpoint `GET /sync/track/:id` returning a binary stream of the audio file, and `GET /sync/cover/:id` returning the cover art.
4. Expose the server's local IP address and port to the React frontend so we can render it as a QR code.
```

### Prompt 2.3 — Flutter Client Sync Module 🟣 CLAUDE CODE
```text
Write a Wi-Fi sync client in the Flutter application.
1. Add `mobile_scanner` to scan the desktop sync QR code (which yields the local IP address/port of the server).
2. Fetch the sync manifest from `http://<ip>:<port>/sync/manifest`.
3. Compare the manifest with the local SQLite records to determine missing tracks.
4. Request missing audio and cover art files sequentially, saving them to the device's application documents directory.
5. Insert metadata records into the local SQLite database.
```
